item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I know someone named Olivia Walsh. Or is it Welsh? I don't know her well, obviously. Anyway, really good work, had me hooked from start to finish.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I noticed a small problem — the source link for the first image (Cliffs of Moher) was disabled. Apparently the photographer has deleted the image from Flickr.

Normally Creative Commons license cannot be revoked, but the discussion page does not specify under what kind of license the first image was originally released so far. If you have a screenshot of the image license at the time you posted this article, or something like that, would you mind sharing it with us?

If you don't have such a thing, I would like to suggest looking for an alternative image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i dont know what to say other than that this article is kind of haunting. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well said, these are my exact thoughts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I….think I liked it? This was definitely a weird article. I can't get the mental image of a body being impaled on the rocks out of my head. It's horrible. Which means that you accomplished your goal I guess. So pretty good writing… I guess. Ugh. Makes me feel gross. Again, seems to be what you wanted.

I do wish there was a little bit more explanation for the actual anomalous effect itself. What was up with that ending? And when Rainbow would suddenly start talking all smart? A little more hints in the article or even just an author post in the comments would do a lot for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if I get it at all. So many people praising the article, but I really saw nothing so worthwhile to read. To me it was very subpar.

Sincerely I don't think most of the article makes sense. Not because of the narrative, but the way it is written and the decisions made just don't sound like the Foundation to me, simply because they don't sound like professional to me.

Maybe if the article was rearranged just for the investigation to be all Abagail's own and not even found out by the Foundation before its end, then it would work better for me, although even then it would be just an ok article.

But who can argue against more than 200 upvotes? Something here worked for a lot of people. To me it is an easy downvote.

PS.: Well written, though, and I would love to see more simple human behaviour like in this one, this part was really well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had the very same thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The experience isn't less corporeal at all.

Olivia went up and, since she probably didn't questioned herself, stayed up to the very end.

Lin went up, met the entity, doubted it and opened her eyes. When she opened her eyes she fell before the very end of the phenomena.

Keep in mind this last log is a video feed recorded in her computer. Lin saw nothing of the description, it was the actual camera. Only after she doubts the entity does she open her eyes and see anything at all. And as it seems, what she sees is something the camera can't get because of the light from behind the entity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw the ending coming, but the logs were a perfect lead up so it still ended with some impact. I do a lot of studying of cults and how they form, and about the only thing I can say this could benefit from is a little bit more cult setup. A bit of love-bombing by Rainbow and OneLeap members would've upped the creepy factor but this is still fantastic.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1. When I saw the author I knew I was in for a good read, and damn I was right. The presentation of the characters is excellent, and that last log is really quite chilling. Superb work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shit, my dude, those are some fuckin' airwaves if I ever saw them. That early video transcript was thoroughly chilling, and the more Rainbow talked, the more I hated him. It was massive watching these poor people get sucked into his bizarre world. And I almost have to wonder if there's actually an anomaly here… I suppose the video evidence is what proves there is. +1

item.title: Re: Small typo
item.content:encoded: Ah, good catch. Fixed!

item.title: Small typo
item.content:encoded: Should be "faze" not "phase"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of my new favourite skips. It has the captivating simplicity of a classic article, slowing building interest with its mystery, and the reports are a well-executed narrative hook.

I also have a soft spot for skips which are phenomena that's just "out there" for anyone who seeks it, and thus cannot be easily contained or restricted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So I read this yesterday, and I wasn't really going to comment on it before I realized that it was quite literally keeping me up at night. When I come to the wiki, I'm not actually expecting to get scared, I'm mostly expecting some fun world-building stories and maybe some fun light-horror concept works to theorize and talk about. But this article fucked me up. Very few articles have really gotten under my skin - ones that come to mind immediately are 2740, 3935, and 3930 - and I think this one gets under mine for a lot of the same reasons but in a completely different and unique way. There's a lot of article cliches here, but they come together in a way that's really unexpected and it all comes together really well. Easiest +1 of my life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to know, was this at all inspired by the concept of flying presented in the Hitchhiker’s Guide to the Galaxy books, which is to “throw yourself at the ground and miss”?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm picking up good vibrations

She's giving me excitations…

+1, very well done

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely phenomenal SCP. I don't really have anything else to say; this is just masterfully crafted and amazing and perfectly done. Kudos, Captain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beautiful, like an angel with a bloody axe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this! I had a sense of unease throughout the article, but Rainbow had almost convinced me until Lin took the leap. +1 from me.

I am confused about some stuff though. For instance, if Lin's body was found on the rocks, how did the camera record anything in the sky? And if her experience was less corporeal than we thought, why can't Olivia's body be found? Or why do they need to create duplicate bodies?

Lin's body being found is the real confusing thing, but I enjoyed this nontheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you enjoyed! Just wanted to note though that two of these are intentional. I called it "Dinine Radiation" on purpose. It's just another part of Rainbow's whole shtick. As for An Garda Síochána, that is actually the correct article use as you can see here. But you are correct about the obtaining/attaining thing. Thanks for that catch!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this one. Great way to come back to the SCP wiki after a long time away.

+1

I did notice a few minor spelling and grammar things you might want to fix:

Incorrect article usage.

An Garda Síochána confirmed that Cucher had indeed died during the incident.

This sentence should use "obtaining" instead of "attaining".

After attaining his address and contact information, Researcher Lin volunteered to interview Rainbow under the guise of a civilian seeking to learn about OneLeap.

I think a typo swapped out a "v" in favour of an "n".

With the invention of Q-Learning and exploration strategies, we see a 300% decrease in the Dinine Radiation that chatbot AIs give off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What a wild ride!

Loving how human Lin felt, and the strangeness of Rainbow and OneJump! Makes me wonder who Rainbow was, and how could he have figured out this phenomena and it leaves me questioning what happened at that bittersweet ending.

But at the same time, it still felt complete and i didn't feel cheated. Definitely one of the better of the new series IMO!

+1, my gal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, I was really vibing with Rainbow. Great story, great creepy end.

The whole cult thing reminded me of this silly short film I was in in high school. Funny cult leader, funny concept, but people still end up dead.

Quickest +1 in a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I'm glad you enjoyed!

I hope to see more of your work on this page

I actually have an author page with all of the stuff I've written on it, so if you want to read more you can see what I've written here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just have to leave a little message here because this story is just amazing to me. Easy +1. I hope to see more of your work on this page, that was REALLY good!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last log was creepy as hell and tied up everything neatly for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The camera was mounted as a bodycam, but the file itself was found on the computer. I don't explain how the file got from the camera to the computer on purpose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very very good, easy +1.

I'm a bit confused as to the position of the camera in the logs. Is it meant to be a bodycam or set up on the cliffs like a tripod? How did the Foundation recover the logs?

item.title: Damn what a twist
item.content:encoded: The article had me by the neck from the first entry in the log. As narrative as it is, it probably would fit equally well as a tale. I'd certainly like to see more of this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was an absolute treat to read; from the moment Rainbow came in I new it was gonna be one of my new favourite articles, and the ending did not disappoint.

+1, and good airwaves.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Bliss is just one jump away. Will you take the leap?

I've been sitting on this one for a few months now, finally decided to put it out there.

Thanks to Mortos, magnadeus, aismallard, The Great Hippo and Vezaz for providing feedback.

And an extra special thank you to Woedenaz for both providing feedback and making the poster! (It looks so fucking good doesn't it?)

Moher Cliffs Image: https://www.flickr.com/photos/19966030@N00/513623724

Poster provided by Woedenaz.

Good Airwaves.
